# Spec: Add Task
<!-- spec-kit 4-layer template · mobile-sdd                         -->

---

## Layer 1 — Business Requirement (BR)
<!-- Why are we building this? Written for PO and stakeholders.      -->
<!-- No implementation detail. No UI. No tech stack.                 -->

### BR-001: Allow users to add tasks to their personal todo list

#### Goal
Users can record a new task in under 3 seconds from the moment they open the app. Every task they type survives across app restarts — no data is ever lost on close.

#### Success Metric
- **time-to-add**: ≤3 seconds from cold open to task appearing in the list (measured by stopwatch on first-tap-to-saved latency)
- **data-durability**: zero tasks lost across 10 consecutive app restart cycles at any list size (empty, 1 task, 50 tasks)

#### Out of Scope
- Task editing (changing the title after creation) — deferred to a later feature
- Task categories, tags, or priorities — not part of the "just a list" vision
- Subtasks or checklist items inside a task — would add nesting that contradicts the simple list model

---

## Layer 2 — Use Cases (UC)
<!-- Who does what with the system?                                   -->
<!-- One section per use case. PO can still read and correct this.   -->
<!-- AI reads this to understand behaviour before writing code.       -->

### UC-01: Add a new task

**Actor:** App user (anyone who opens the app)
**Trigger:** User wants to remember or track something they need to do

**Main Flow:**
1. User sees a text input at the top of the screen with a placeholder "What needs to be done?"
2. User taps the input and types the task title using the on-screen keyboard
3. User presses the add button (labeled "+" or "Add") to confirm
4. System saves the task and adds it to the top of the visible list
5. The input clears and focus remains available for the next task
6. The task appears with an unchecked checkbox and the title the user typed

**Alternative Flows:**
- None

**Exceptions:**
- **E1. Empty input**: User presses the add button with no text entered. The system does nothing — no empty task is created, no error is shown. The input keeps focus.
- **E2. Whitespace-only input**: User enters only spaces and presses add. The system trims the input and treats it as E1 — no task is created.
- **E3. Very long title**: User types more than 500 characters. The system still saves the full text but displays the title truncated at 200 characters in the list view, showing the full text on tap or via ellipsis.

---

## Layer 3 — Entity Model
<!-- What are the domain nouns?                                       -->
<!-- Not an ERD. No datatypes, no indexes, no foreign keys yet.      -->
<!-- Focus on names, meanings, states, and relationships.            -->
<!-- Use the exact terms the team will use in code.                  -->

### Task
- **Represents:** A single actionable item the user wants to track
- **States:** `active` (not yet completed), `completed` (done)
- **Fields (conceptual):**
  - `id` — stable UUID assigned at creation, never changes
  - `title` — the free-text description the user typed
  - `completed` — whether the task is done (always `false` at creation; toggled by a separate feature)
  - `createdAt` — timestamp of when the task was first saved
  - `updatedAt` — timestamp of the last change to this task
- **Relationships:**
  - Belongs to: the app's single task list (the user's personal collection)

---

## Layer 4 — Acceptance Criteria (AC)
<!-- How do we know it is done correctly?                             -->
<!-- One AC per scenario. Each AC maps to exactly one Maestro flow.  -->
<!-- Every AC MUST have a → e2e: forward link.                       -->
<!-- No two ACs may share the same flow file path.                   -->

### UC-01: Add a new task

#### AC-1: User adds a task with a valid title
→ e2e: `e2e/flows/add-task/UC-01-add-task/AC-1-add-valid-task.yaml`
→ test-name: `UC-01_AC-1_add-valid-task`

**Given:** The app is open and ready
**When:** The user types "Buy groceries" into the text input and presses the add button
**Then:** The task "Buy groceries" appears at the top of the list with an unchecked checkbox
**And:** The text input is cleared and ready for the next entry

#### AC-2: User tries to add an empty task
→ e2e: `e2e/flows/add-task/UC-01-add-task/AC-2-reject-empty-task.yaml`
→ test-name: `UC-01_AC-2_reject-empty-task`

**Given:** The app is open and the text input is empty
**When:** The user presses the add button
**Then:** No new task appears in the list
**And:** The input stays focused and empty

#### AC-3: User adds a task with a very long title
→ e2e: `e2e/flows/add-task/UC-01-add-task/AC-3-long-title-handled.yaml`
→ test-name: `UC-01_AC-3_long-title-handled`

**Given:** The app is open and ready
**When:** The user types a title of 500 characters into the text input and presses the add button
**Then:** The task appears in the list (display text may be truncated or ellipsized)
**And:** The full text is saved and retrievable

#### AC-4: Task persists after app restart
→ e2e: `e2e/flows/add-task/UC-01-add-task/AC-4-task-survives-restart.yaml`
→ test-name: `UC-01_AC-4_task-survives-restart`

**Given:** The user has added a task "Buy groceries" to the list
**When:** The app is closed and reopened
**Then:** The task "Buy groceries" is still visible in the list with an unchecked checkbox
